From:Barbara McCullough < HYPERLINK "mailto:bbmccullough@brighter-beginnings.org" bbmccullough@brighter-beginnings.org>
Sent:Wednesday, April 03, 2013 12:44 PM
To: HYPERLINK "mailto:Steven@meetthere.com" Steven@meetthere.com
Cc:Lonnie Dillard
Subject:RE: Consulting 

Hello Steven, and Thanks so much Lonnie!

 

I am really pleased that you have agreed to take some of your precious “retirement” time to help us sort out what we need.  To expand on what Lonnie has sent you, I really need to also sort out what we need for client database “leadership” and how the MIS work structure/system should interface with the IT structure/system.  Here is my summary of what we are trying to figure out with the MIS side:

 

I think what we really need is someone who can help us problem solve what we really need (the type of talents) to effectively LEAD the following processes:

1.    Developing and designing programs in ETO to meet our needs (and/or creating the specs for Social Solutions to create)

2.    Creating reports for managers and staff that help us track performance of staff and outcomes for clients (this is the big one because report design seems to be even harder than creating screens)

3.    Data integrity, routine and regular methods for finding if there are data entry problems and problem solving the best fixes for maintaining data integrity

4.    Data integration – we have multiple ETO databases, plus multiple other databases which makes even the simplest report, such as how many client we serve and their basic demographics, almost impossible to do.

5.  Electronic Health Record – is on the horizon with yet another database.  This will only complicate the issues with #4.

 

Here are some dates to meet or talk via phone:  Fri March 5 between noon and 2 (phone), Monday 4/8 I am open to meet or talk/phone between noon and 5.  Wed 4/10 just about any time.

 

Do any of these work for you?  Again, thank you so much for taking the time to help us sort through the best approach for creating a cohesive and functional IT/MIS system.

 

Barbara Bunn McCullough, Ph.D.
Executive Director
Brighter Beginnings
Oakland, CA 94601
Ph:  510-903-7503
Fax:  510-842-0291

Barbara:

 

I spoke to Steven today and he said that he is interested in helping us on some projects.  His email address is HYPERLINK "mailto:Steven@meetthere.com"Steven@meetthere.com.  Given your schedule, I thought the best thing would be to have you contact him to set up a meeting.  I have given him a lot of background, including the information below, attached and the notes to yesterday’s meeting.  I can be available (except 9 – 11 Thursday) if you want me to be involved.

 

Thanks, Lonnie

 

From: Lonnie S. Dillard [mailto:lsdillard1@cs.com] 
Sent: Monday, April 01, 2013 1:01 PM
To: 'Steven dosRemedios'
Cc: 'Lonnie Dillard'
Subject: RE: Consulting 

 

Steve:

 

Brighter Beginnings (formerly the Perinatal Council) is a non-profit organization which was started almost 30 years ago to provide social services to pregnant and parenting teens and their babies, though the work has expanded to children and their families.  

 

Currently, there are 72 employees.  Much of the work is done through home visits and other outreach, therefore many staff are in the field much of the time.  Over the years, governmental funding agencies have required that more and more data be reported for statistical analysis and measurement of outcomes.

 

Toward this end, we implemented new information systems this year under the direction of a consultant who is no longer in country.   We recently purchased 20 new laptops and the remaining lapops are 2 to 3 years old.  We have a mid-level IT manager on staff and a staff that works part time on IT input and reporting.  

 

Attached are 3 documents which will give you some background on our systems.  The implementation was rocky but has essentially been completed and is pretty much in accordance with the proposals.  

 

Problems that have not been solved by current IT staff:

·         Chronic phone outages at 2648 & 2595 International

·         Chronic wireless problems at 2595 International and 2727 MacDonald.

·         Remote connection issues

·         Complaints about sluggish and non working laptops.

·         Poorly implemented Outlook and Sharepoint.

·         Lack of training staff in new technology.

 

 

IT/Phone/Infrastructure needs as described by the ED:

·         Analyze and remotely fix computers and laptops malfunctions.

·         Analyze and correct network problems related to accessing printers (EHS Oakland) and using the wireless and cell phones in the  Richmond office, for example.

·         Project manage vendors such as MegaPath and Sprint, and escalate to upper levels within the vendor when appropriate.

·         Train staff to use our equipment and keep it usable.

·         Project future hardware and software needs.

 

We also have ETO (Efforts To Outcomes) proprietary software which needs data collection and reporting enhancements, as well as integration with other applications (probably through import/export).  I can give you more information on ETO if you wish.

 

Thanks for taking a look.

 

Lonnie

On Sun, Mar 23, 2014 at 8:40 AM, Lyn Hunter <lhunter@lbl.gov> wrote:

barb singleton, natalie roe, and i are trying to pull together a group to go watch a new documentary called "particle fever" this friday, at shattuck cinema, at 7:30 p.m.

as some of you may be aware, natalie heads up the physics division at berkeley lab, and many of her researchers have been involved with the subject of the movie...finding the elusive higgs boson particle. 

we thought it would be nice to get a group together to watch the film, then after head over to a local establishment and have natalie talk with us a bit about what we saw, if it works with people's schedules. 

a fun and social way to enhance our science knowledge, yes?!!

let me know if you are interested in attending. and please forward this e-mail to anyone else who think might be interested, including significant others.

below is a description of the film from the SF chronicle review, as well as a link to the particle fever trailer. thanks.

The Higgs boson isn't the God particle, but it's about as fundamental as anything can get, and discovering it required the biggest experiment in the history of science. It makes an intriguing documentary.
Two scientists - David Kaplan, professor of particle physics at Johns Hopkins, and Mark Levinson, a filmmaker with a doctorate in physics from UC Berkeley - have produced "Particle Fever," a drama of the experiment's real people in failure and triumph, and an intimate look at the awesome world of Big Science.
It's 99 minutes long, but worth it, and have no fear of heavy stuff: There is none.
full review: http://www.sfgate.com/default/article/Particle-Fever-an-intimate-look-at-a-weighty-5295703.php

Summary: See All About It | Vivian Maier's Newspaper Portraits
Location: North Gate Hall: Hearst Avenue @ Euclid Avenue, Berkeley, CA, U
Description:
You are cordially invited to an opening reception and lecture to celebrate the newly endowed and named Reva and David Logan Gallery of Documentary Photography in the halls of the UC Berkeley Graduate School of Journalism.

Starting in the late 1940s, Vivian Maier, an unmarried nanny in Chicago, obsessively took more than 100,000 photographs documenting everyday life in the mid-twentieth century. Not seen until she died in 2009, her work soon became a worldwide Internet sensation. She is now considered one of the century’s most accomplished street photographers.

This exhibit, of never-before-seen photographs Maier took of the daily newspapers of her hometown Chicago, was curated from the Jeffrey Goldstein collection and honors a major gift to the Graduate School of Journalism from the Reva and David Logan Foundation.

Photography professor Ken Light worked for years with David and his son Jonathan, to bring the late David Logan’s exceptional and rare collection of photography books to UC’s Bancroft Library. A $3.1 million bequest from the Logan family also established a chaired professorship and funding to ensure that North Gate will remain a regular venue for exhibitions which Ken will continue to curate.

This exhibition celebrates the gift from the Logan family, also from Chicago.  At newsstands, on the street, tucked under an arm, delivered on a bike, or scattered in the wind, newspaper front pages shouted for attention and could capture the mood or energy of the day.

Summary: Connect with Doug re BEACON Site
Robert Nickelsberg | Afghanistan: A Distant War
Location: North Gate Hall: Hearst Avenue @ Euclid Avenue, Berkeley, CA, U
Description:
Robert Nickelsberg captures the brutality and suffering, often amid stunning beauty, of the convulsions that have engulfed Afghanistan. Here he presents an extraordinary portfolio of images, accompanied by incisive commentary, that explain the country's tragic history.

Nickelsberg, a TIME magazine contract photographer for 25 years, was based in New Delhi from 1988 to 2000. During that time, he documented conflicts in Kashmir, Iraq, Sri Lanka, India and Afghanistan. He was one of the few photographers who had first hand exposure to the early days of the rise of fundamentalist groups in the Afghanistan-Pakistan tribal areas and al-Qaeda, and his work provides a unique up close view of the Soviet withdrawal, the rise of the Taliban and the invasion by the U.S.

Nickelsberg moved to New York in 2000 and continues to travel overseas - reporting on the invasion of Afghanistan in 2001 and Iraq in 2003 - and focus on chronicling the devastating psychological effects of war in Kashmir.

In 2008, he was awarded grants from the Dart Center for Journalism and Trauma, and from the South Asia Journalists Association to document and report on post-traumatic stress disorder in Kashmir after 20 years of insurgency. Nickelsberg serves on the advisory board of the Kashmir Initiative at the Carr Center for Human Rights Policy at Harvard University.

ISM-NC November 5, 2009

 Dinner Meeting Announcement

Non Members and Guests Welcome

 

 

“Strategic Sourcing 

Transformation at Clorox”

 

with

 

Scott Wilkerson
Director
Global Strategic Sourcing
The Clorox Company



Program Summary 
  
At The Clorox Company, strategic sourcing is core to winning in the aggressively competitive consumer goods space.  Wall Street looks to Clorox to deliver $90-100 million in year-over-year structural savings; this last fiscal year was a record for the company.  The sourcing organization's scorecard is also core to driving results in supplier diversity, product quality and product availability. 

Clorox is implementing the next phase of its transformation plan, which includes changes to how the sourcing team works with R&D, manufacturing, the business units and suppliers.  In concert with these changes, Clorox has also partnered with a sourcing services firm, ICG Commerce, to augment efforts for Indirect spend categories. 

Scott Wilkerson will discuss the design framework, the new operating model, and lessons learned. 
  

Transformation roadmap for Global Strategic Sourcing 
Operating model:  the "What" and the "How" 
Partnering with a third-party sourcing services firm: status of the market, scope and services, findings 
Lessons learned

 



Biography of the Speaker

Scott is the Director of Sourcing at The Clorox Company.  Scott is responsible for sourcing all indirect goods and services – including marketing, sales, HR, professional services, transportation, warehousing, capital and MRO – for North America.  Scott is also responsible for the Strategic Capabilities Group, which leads for Global Strategic Sourcing performance reporting, technology planning, sourcing processes and training. 

Scott is a board member of Family Support Services of the Bay Area, which provides comprehensive services to children in high-risk circumstances. 

Scott is a graduate of the University of California, Berkeley.

What is the Market Economy or the Free Enterprise System?
 Four Characteristics of the economy - how it effects employment
 
Are your skills transferable within the Market Economy?

 The C.P.M. (Certified Purchasing Manager)
 
Bio. of the Speaker: Jim Hays has over 20 years experience in management and purchasing/supply management profession as a U. S. Air Force Officer, government warranted contracting officer and private sector buyer, supervisor and manager. He has served as executive director for NAPM-NC since 1988.  Over the past several years he has taught Supply Chain Management courses at CSU-Hayward. For 15 years he has been teaching purchasing and supply chain management courses for U.C. Berkeley Extension, San Francisco State University, De Anza College and InfoTech, a management consulting firm in Mexico City. He has conducted training courses for the U.S. Postal Service, Pacific Bell/SBC, AT&T, Syntex Corp., PG&E, Bank of America, Visa International, and Lam Research.  He has taught C.P.M. Review courses for PG&E, the U.S. Postal Services, Lam Research and CSU-Hayward Extended Education. Jim holds a BA from the University of Wyoming and an MA from San Francisco State University. Some personal notes: He's a recovering former military intelligence officer, a fact that informs and colors this presentation.

Strategic Sourcing Transformation at Clorox

Presented by 


Julia M. Brown
VP- Corporate Procurement and Contract Manufacturing
The Clorox Services Company

Julia Brown began her career with Procurement with Procter & Gamble in Toronto, Canada in 1991 after earning an honors Bachelor of Commerce degree from McMaster University in Hamilton, Ontario,Canada.

From 1991 until 1995, Julia held a variety of positions in Procurement at Procter & Gamble Canada with increasing global responsibilities in Fabric and Home Care and Marketing Procurement. She transitioned to P&G’s global headquarters in Cincinnati in 1995, post a global restructuring where she continued to develop her global Procurement Career in P&G’s Baby Care and Beauty (Baltimore, MD) businesses. 

In 2001, Julia left P&G to assume the position of Director of Integration at Diageo- a premium spirits and wine company in Stamford, CT. She was responsible for co-leading the Procurement integration of the Seagram’s company into Diageo. After completing that work, she assumed two combined newly created positions to build world class capability in procurement as well as to lead their Logistics Procurement strategy. 

In January 2004, Julia left Diageo to assume a combined position of two newly created positions at Gillette in Boston, MA. As a Global Director, she was accountable for creating and driving global strategy in Gillette’s Global Direct Materials spend across all business units. In this capacity, she also created an organization to integrate and manage a newly acquired oral care business. She was also the Director of the Strategic Sourcing Center of Excellence for Gillette which developed and deployed processes, systems and tools to build world class capability in Procurement. In this additional capacity, she built Gillette’s Supplier Diversity program to win regional Corporation of the Year in 2004 and served as co-chair of the New England Minority Supplier Development Council board of directors.

Julia joined The Clorox Company in October 2005 as Vice President of Corporate Procurement and Contract Manufacturing in Oakland, CA. She joined the Junior Achievement Board of Directors in November 2005.

Sent:Thursday, June 28, 2007 3:10 PM
Subject:Members Only Movie Screening of Arctic Tale!

Attachments:image001.jpg; image002.jpg

Dear Chabot Members,

Because you support Chabot by being a member, you, your family, and friends are invited to attend a very special screening of the new movie, Artic Tale here at Chabot Space & Science Center on Tuesday, July 10th at 7pm. Plus there’s an after movie Q&A with the director Sarah Robertson!

 

Narrated by Queen Latifah,  Artic Tale,  is an epic adventure documentary that explores the vast world of the Great North and follows the life of a Polar Bear cub and a Walrus cub as they learn about life from their mothers and have to deal with the catastrophic changes in the Arctic weather caused by Global Warming. The film has a broad appeal for young and old alike and is funny, exciting, sad, and touching all at the same time.  

 

Screening Date: Tuesday, July 10th

Screening Time:  7pm

Q&A with Director immediately following at 8:23pm

 

Refreshments will be available for purchase prior to the film screening.

 

Space will be limited - RSVP is REQUIRED by Monday, July 2nd 5pm

or call: 510-336-7421 

 

Chabot Space & Science Center is located at 10000 Skyline Blvd. Directions may be found at HYPERLINK "http://www.chabotspace.org/directions"www.chabotspace.org/directions

What is National Night Out?
National Night Out is the nation's night out against crime. Sponsored by the National Association of Town Watch, residents are encouraged to join with their neighbors to have a neighborhood block party, ice cream social or other outdoor event to build neighborhood spirit and unity - which is the first defense against crime. Research shows that when neighbors know each other and look out for each other crime goes down.

 

The Kingwood Road Neighborhood Watch will be holding a block party on August 4, 2009 from 7-9 PM in front of number 20 (the Kessler home) again. Please bring something to share along with a chair. A good time, great fun and getting to know your neighbors!

 



  
We will be paid a visit by the OPD. Usually they pass out some goodies for kids and adults alike. MARK YOUR CALENDAR FOR AUGUST 4TH!

 

Best regards,

 


Jerry & Esther Moran


30 Kingwood Rd.

510-530-9342

The Sarbanes-Oxley Experience
Presented by

Darryl Sudduth
VP Corporate Procurement, Williams-Sonoma
And
Samuel Schiff
Strategic Sourcing Manager 
University of California, Berkeley
 
 
 
PROGRAM SUMMARY
 
· The Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002, SAS 70, section 404, section 409 and the COSO framework defined.
 
· Outline of the process and methodology used for phase I.
 
· Results of the process from procurement to settlement and  other byproducts derived from the  exercise.
 
· Strategic Sourcing focus of the Sarbanes-Oxley experience.
 
Biographies of the Presenters (Both members of NAPM-NC)
 
Darryl Sudduth is the Vice President of Corporate Procurement for Williams-Sonoma, Inc., a national retailer of specialty products for the home. He is currently responsible for all non-merchandise procurement activities for all store and corporate locations in the US and Canada.  Darryl has over twenty years experience in retail and manufacturing procurement for major US corporations, including Dole Food Co., Nestle Beverage Co., Foster Farms, MJB Coffee and Williams-Sonoma.  He has participated in the implementation of operating systems such as SAP R2 and R3, JD Edwards and Lawson 8.03. He was also a member of the steering committee and implementation team for the VMR Program (strategic sourcing initiative) at Nestle USA in the mid ‘90’s. Over the last 14 months, he has concentrated on the implementation of SAS 70, section 404 Sarbanes-Oxley financial control process guidelines and developed standard operating procedures for Williams-Sonoma’s procurement and settlement functions.
 
Samuel Schiff is a Strategic Sourcing Manager at University of California, Berkeley. He manages the sourcing, implementation, contract compliance, and supplier performance monitoring of  strategic agreements for key commodities and services. He has over 15 years of experience in strategic sourcing, procurement and supply chain management consulting. Prior to joining UC Berkeley, Mr. Schiff was an Executive Consultant with IBM Global Services in the Supply Chain Management Practice. Prior to IBM, Mr. Schiff was a Director in the Product Value Chain Practice of Adario, a global venture consultancy spun off from SRI International.  Before joining Adario, Mr. Schiff was a Senior Manager with Arthur D. Little, Inc. where he focused on strategic sourcing and procurement in the Operations Management Practice

NAPM-NC November 3, 2005 "Thursday" Evening Dinner Meeting Announcement
 
"Innovative Concepts in Strategic Supply Management"

Presented by
Mark Trowbridge, C.P.M., Principal
Robert Dunn, M.B.A., C.P.M., Principal
Strategic Procurement Solutions
 
The transition towards “Strategic” supply management continues to reach new levels in innovation.  New technologies, techniques, and processes are empowering organizations to achieve remarkable results…far more than “traditional strategic sourcing”.  This presentation will discuss "Innovative Concepts in Strategic Supply Management" which empower  “best practice” performance.
 
Tonight’s presenters work with leading private & public sector procurement groups to improve the performance of their entire supply chain.  They have helped large enterprises save millions of dollars through advanced strategies in sourcing, supplier management, and customer outreach.  Their clients have included:  Apple Computer, Colgate-Palmolive, PacifiCare Health Systems, State of Oregon, Limited Brands, Entergy Services, Sybase, Bristol-Myers Squibb, State of Massachusetts, Vision Service Plan, Union Bank of California, American Savings Bank, State of Illinois, Metropolitan Life Insurance Nationwide Insurance, Allstate Insurance, Cooper Tire & Rubber, and Huntington Banks.
 
This presentation will discuss evolutionary stages in the growth of supply chain excellence…in managing sourcing, suppliers, internal customers, and procurement processes. 
 
Robert Dunn, C.P.M., M.B.A.: His career in supply management has covered 40 years.  Mr. Dunn’s consulting experience has included highly successful projects on behalf of three consulting firms.  Previously, Mr. Dunn held key corporate positions in purchasing & materials management in the Government, Technology, & Financial sectors, leading to a position as Director of Corporate Procurement for BankAmerica Corporation. 
 
Mr. Dunn has a dynamic teaching style, and is a popular guest lecturer to clients, college programs & industry associations concerning ‘Best Practices in Procurement’ & related topics.  He has instructed at the collegiate level for 15 years in the field of Purchasing & Materials Management, formerly at Saint Mary’s College in the Contracts Management MBA Program and California State University, Hayward, in the Graduate School of Business & Economics.
 
Bob Dunn is past President of two NAPM chapters in Northern California.  He also chaired the CAPS Executive Board, leading innovative projects with the most respected CPO’s in the country.  Robert received his BS degree from the University of San Francisco School of Business Administration and his MBA from San Francisco University.  Bob also has his CPM certification from the Institute of Supply Management.
 
Mark Trowbridge, C.P.M.:  His 18 years in procurement included corporate positions in the Financial, Airlines, & Manufacturing sectors, most recently serving as Director of Contracting Management for Bank of America…achieving $250 Million in savings through sourcing and key negotiations of traditional and non-traditional acquisitions.
Mark has also experienced great success in procurement consulting, exceeding targeted savings objectives on large sourcing projects in the Telecommunications, Health Care, Banking, & Insurance sectors for two leading consulting firms, and most recently with Strategic Procurement Solutions.  During his consulting tenure, Mr. Trowbridge has assisted clients in capturing more than $175 Million in cost reductions.
Mr. Trowbridge holds a Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration from Pepperdine University. He earned his C.P.M. designation from the ISM in 1993 and his Lifetime Certification in 2003. Mark is currently a member of both the ISM and the NCMA. He is an invited guest speaker for purchasing groups, including numerous seminars for corporate clients and professional associations. 
These gentlemen are both co-founders of Strategic Procurement Solutions (HYPERLINK "http://www.StrategicProcurementSolutions.com"www.StrategicProcurementSolutions.com), a provider of advanced supply management consulting & training services to Fortune 500 corporations and public-sector agencies.  They are frequent guest speakers for professional associations, including many presentations for local N.I.G.P., I.S.M., and N.C.M.A. chapters; and key addresses at the 2002 & 2005 I.S.M. Services Conference (Tempe, Arizona); and featured/scheduled presentations at the 2003, 2004, 2005, and 2006 I.S.M. International Conferences. 
 
Please join us for this timely presentation on predictive supplier performance.

the meeting is below:

vIntroductions

vOverview of event elements

vPast event happenings

vEvent job descriptions

vMewsic 2004 Goals

vNext steps

 
We've identified teams for the event and this is what we have so far:  
Publicity
Sponsorship
Logistics & Entertainment
Food & Beverage
Decorating
Auction & Donations
Invitation & Program
Ticket sales
 
The debriefing meeting from last year went well.  The 2004 event, on Sunday 10/24/04, will be the third time Hopalong has put this event on.  The good news is that we've learned a lot in our first two years!  I'm in the process of putting the steering committee together, and do hope that you will join us.  The steering committee will meet once a month from January through July; 2 meetings in August; 4 meetings in September and 3 meetings in October.  We may scale these meetings back if we find we don't need them (though I think it's better to err on the side of more communication rather than less...).  Because of the various event facets, we will need sub-committees for each area.  The subcommittees members would not need to necessarily attend the steering committee meetings; though a representative of each subcommittee would need to make sure that information on projects and progress was forwarded to the committee chair.  ie - one member of the steering committee acts as the publicity chair, and has a subcommittee of volunteers working with them on publicity.
 
Getting the steering committee members together will be the first task.  Because Hopalong has a volunteer coordinator, once our chairpersons are appointed (that sounds awful formal) on the steering committee, our volunteer coordinator can help us recruit subcommittee members for each area. 
 
The first three steering committee meetings will be: Thursday 1/29; Thursday 2/26; Thursday 3/25.  They will take place at the Hopalong admin offices at 1738 Excelsior Ave., Oakland.
 
Please let me know if you will be able to join us - your help is appreciated on any level that you can give.
 
Rosalie Brown
Development Director
Hopalong Animal Rescue

Job description:

The Kaiser Permanente Procurement & Supply function is establishing best practices in Supply chain. The Sourcing Manager will be part of this implementation by performing all sourcing/procurement activities for the assigned categories; develop and implement a sourcing management strategy for these categories assigned; identify, develop and manage supplier relationships; effectively negotiate contracts and long term procurement agreements; develop and propose metrics to analyze supplier total cost to Kaiser. In addition, participate in the change management programs to establish processes and procedures. These activities are expected to realize significant efficiencies and cost savings. -----Original Message-----
From: Conrad Jung
Sent: Tuesday, March 23, 2004 4:01 PM
To: All CSSC Staff
Subject: Staff Telescope Viewing

Good Afternoon To All,

I tried to send this out last Sunday night, but judging by the conversations that I've had with a number of you the message is stuck inside of our server.  

Staff and volunteers are invited Thursday evening for a night under the stars.  Some of you mentioned that you couldn't make the last time so I hope you can make this time. 

The Observatory will open about 6:00 and viewing will go to about 9. 

For those of you that are trying to think of whether or not to be there, I have some thoughts:

1)  If the weather is good, and it was the last time we did this, you get a great sunset--and it will be over the Golden Gate Bridge!

2)  This will be an opportunity to see in the sky all 5 of the classically known planets.  You know them,  Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn.  

Though Thursday is not the only evening to see all 5 of these planets, if you wait too long, let say the 1st week in April, Mercury will be missing from the this evening collection of planets.  To see all 5 of the classically known planets, look in the sky about 20-30 minutes after sunset.  Incidentally, the next time this is supposed occur in the evening sky is the spring of 2036.  

3)  Saturn.  This is one of the scene stealers of the night sky.  If you haven't seen this in the telescope or you weren't able to make it to the last Staff Viewing, you have to see this!  OK, I may be a bit biased, but ask your office mate near you who was at the last Staff Viewing.  

Just another point about Saturn.  Right now the view of the rings is about as good as it gets.  Though Saturn's rings are visible most of the time we look at it, its position in the sky is not always this favorable.  It takes about 30 years for the ring angle and the planet location to repeat.  So, what are you doing in 2034!

4)  The Moon.  The other big scene stealer of the night sky.  It will be a crescent in the evening sky.  If the air is clear and calm, the experience will be the next thing to being an Apollo astronaut.  Also that night, the Moon will be in conjunction with Mars.  It will be seen as the starlike object underneath the crescent Moon.  

5)  This is an opportunity to get out and relax a little.

This invitation is extended to friends and relatives that you might like to bring.  

Though the last time this was done the weather was great, I make no promises as to what the weather will be this coming Thursday.  If it's clear then the Observatory will be open.  Fog, thick clouds, drizzle, or worse will cancel this event.

So, hoping for good weather and seeing you all out there,


Conrad Jung

Chabot Space & Science Center

10000 Skyline Blvd.

Oakland, CA   94619

cjung@chabotspace.org 


PS:  The latest weather forecast isn't very promising, but I'm going to try and keep an optimistic outlook on this.  If this date doesn't work I'll reschedule it.

PRESENTATION OUTLINE

All Procurement teams are really doing the same things, with the only variables being how far advanced they are, and how well they are doing them

Biography of the Speaker:
Clive R. Heal is Head of Global Procurement for Genentech Inc. Clive joined Genentech in October 2003 and is responsible for managing the company’s Strategic Sourcing and Procurement Services teams, responsible for $2 billion annual expenditure. Prior to joining Genentech, Clive served as Head of Group Procurement at Prudential plc in the United Kingdom, and as Director and Vice President European Procurement at SmithKline Beecham (now GlaxoSmithKline). Clive was also a management consultant at Price Waterhouse Coopers, following positions in Procurement, Sales, Quality Control, and Research & Development at Reckitt Benkeiser. Clive has worked extensively in Europe and America, plus various assignments in India, Australia, Singapore and Mexico. Clive is a Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Purchasing & Supply (CIPS), and served as Vice Chairman and Chairman-elect of CIPS as well as Chairman of the CIPS Best Practice Group. He has worked extensively in Europe and the United States, and has been appointed a “UK Leading Light” – one of the top 20 UK Procurement Directors as determined by Supply Management. 

He is currently Chairman of the ISM Committee on Corporate Social Responsibility, a visionary, and member of the Center for Strategic Supply Leadership. Clive holds a Bachelor of Science Honours degree in Chemistry from the University of Leicester, England, with minors in Economics and Mathmatics.

Summary: Best of Broadway: Kiss Me Kate

Description:
The work of Life Magazine photographers is a record of the history of American photojournalism as well as the changing face of the world in the last half of the 20th century. JON BRENNEIS, LOOMIS DEAN, WAYNE MILLER, and JOE MUNROE were among the first wave of LIFE photographers. 

The Pacific Center for the Photographic Arts invites you to a panel discussion.

WHEN LIFE WAS YOUNG: JON BRENNEIS, LOOMIS DEAN, WAYNE MILLER AND JOE MUNROE

Saturday, February 28, 4 p.m.
Pacific Center for the Photographic Arts
4221 Hollis St. (at Park Ave.) in Emeryville, across from Pixar Animation Studios.

Free to PCPA members
Others: recommended $10 donation
RSVP: Phone: (510) 428-9169
Mstreshinsky@earthlink.net

The event will include a first-hand account of what it was like to work for LIFE in those heady, early years of photojournalism, a selection of their photographs, a slide show  and a chance to meet the lively four who are considered legends in their field. 

They took their cameras off to World War II,  chronicled the demise of the family farm, made regular forays into Hollywood, traveled with presidents and premiers.  Along the way they worked for most of the major photo magazines of the era, produced films, books, and shows, traveled the world, and had a fine time of it.

*JON BRENNEIS turned out covers for magazines like LIFE and SCIENTIFIC AMERICAN, as well as a body of corporate work that took him around the globe several times.

*LOOMIS DEAN, joined the staff of  LIFE, and produced hundreds of covers and photographed coveys of Hollywood starlets, sports stars.

*WAYNE MILLER¹s work includes the recently published ³Chicago¹s Southside²1946-1948, University of California Press 2000,

*JOE MUNROE chronicled, on film, the rise and fall of the family farm from the l930s to the l990s.

Books and prints will be available.
Courtyard Marriott

Sylvia,

How timely this sweet message is!  I was just going to sit down and FINALLY email you.  I'm coming out of a dark cave, after months of intensive, dreadfully long hours at work, and at last, feeling like a human being, I was going to write you and suggest that we get together.  For anything.  A movie, drinks after work, visit a museum, just do something together the 2 of us.  This is the first time in probably 6 months that I'm not all stressed out from work.  My project was approved last week at the Port Commission (YEAH!), and although it's not ready to close escrow and we still have lots of work to do, my miserable 16-hour days, 7 days a week, are over.  

 

So, would you like to see "My One and Only" with Renee Z. at the Cerrito speakeasy?  This is the theater that serves food and has big comfy chairs.  I'm available any evening next week.  The Informant looks good, too if you prefer.

 

It was great to hear from you, and the message is so true.  You are a special friend-please forgive me for being so incommunicado for so long.  Those days are OVER!! Retirement beckons soon!       
Jennifer


----- Original Message -----
From: "Sylvia dosRemedios" <Sylvia@meetThere.com>
To: "Sylvia dosRemedios" <Sylvia@meetThere.com>
Sent: Friday, September 18, 2009 6:20:13 PM GMT -08:00 US/Canada Pacific
Subject: Sisters


A young wife sat on a sofa on a hot humid day,drinking iced tea and
visiting with her mother. As they talked about life, about marriage,
about the
responsibilities of life and the obligations of adulthood, the mother
clinked the ice cubes in her glass thoughtfully and turned a clear,
sober glance
upon her daughter..

'Don't forget your sisters,' she advised, swirling the tea leaves to
the bottom of her glass. 'They'll
be more important as you get older. No matter how much you love your
husband, no matter how much you
love the children you may have, you are still going to need sisters.
Remember to go places with them now
and then; do things with them..'

'Remember that 'sisters' means ALL the women...your girlfriends, your
daughters, and all your other
women relatives too. 'You'll need other women. Women always do.'

What a funny piece of advice!' the young woman thought. Haven't I just
gotten married?
Haven't I just joined the couple-world? I'm now a married woman, for
goodness sake! A grownup! Surely
my husband and the family we may start will be all I need to make my
life worthwhile!'

But she listened to her mother. She kept contact with her sisters and
made more women friends each
year. As the years tumbled by, one after another,she gradually came to
understand that her mother really
knew what she was talking about. As time and nature work their changes
and their mysteries upon a woman,
sisters are the mainstays of her life..

After more than 50 years of living in this world, here is what I've learned:

THIS SAYS IT ALL:

Time passes.

Life happens.

Distance separates.

Children grow up.

Jobs come and go.

Love waxes and wanes.

Men don't do what they're supposed to do.

Hearts break.

Parents die.

Colleagues forget favors.

Careers end.

BUT.........


Sisters are there, no matter how much time and how many miles are
between you. A girl friend is never farther away than needing her can
reach.
When you have to walk that lonesome valley and you have to walk it by
yourself, the women in your life will be on the valley's rim, cheering
you on,
praying for you, pulling for you, intervening on your behalf, and
waiting with open arms at the valley's end.. Sometimes, they will
even break the rules and walk
beside you...Or come in and carry you out.



Girlfriends, daughters, granddaughters, daughters-in-law, sisters,
sisters-in-law, Mothers,Grandmothers, aunties, nieces, cousins, and
extended
family: all bless our life!

The world wouldn't be the same without women, and neither would I.
When we began this adventure called womanhood, we had no idea of the
incredible joys or
sorrows that lay ahead. Nor did we know how much we would need each
other..Every day, we need each other still.



Pass this on to all the women who help make your life meaningful.

I just did. Short and very sweet:

There are more than twenty angels in this world.
Ten are peacefully sleeping on clouds.

Nine are playing.

And one is reading her email at this moment.

Send this message to ten of your friends including me. If you get 5
replies, someone you love will surprise you..
Happy days!

Don't break this; it's working

Sorry - the date in the body of the email is correct - it's Wednesday, Dec. 9. Hope to see you there!


Photo Contest Reception
Wednesday, December 9, 2009
5:30 – 7pm
Lounge at Scott’s Seafood, Jack London Square

 
OCVB is hosting a casual reception for the participating photographers and guests to view the winning images from the 2009 Picture Oakland Photo Contest and a sampling from previous years contests.  We will all meet in the lounge at Scotts Restaurant at 5:30 pm and walk over together to debut the images at 6:15. Thanks to Jack London Square Partners for providing the available store front space to exhibit these amazing Oakland images. This year we will present them electronically on a large screen TV so that they can be seen day or night 24/7 for visitors to the square to enjoy. The OCVB has hosted the Picture Oakland online photo contest for the last three years and is thankful to all of those that have contributed their time, and artistic talent to making it so successful.  
 
This is hosted by the OCVB and is by invitation only, however if you would like to bring a guest that is fine, just let us know when you RSVP.  
RSVP by December 2, 2009. 
 
Hope to see you there! Please let me know if you have any questions. 

 
Cheers, Elisa for the Oakland Convention & Visitors Bureau

Here is a reminder about the next match:  It is at home on Saturday, May 8 at 2:30 p.m.  We provide food and drinks.

Please note the changes in the line up.  Also, I realize that Linda M., Claudia, April  and Sharon have not yet played.  I hope to begin to get you into the line up in the next few weeks.  In the meantime, Lyn is organizing some practices/scrimmages for us over the next few weeks so that we can get in more play as a team.

 

We will need to make up the rained out match against Mills.  I have a date of June 12 at 12:30 p.m. for that.  Still need to have the agreement of the opposing captain.

 

We are now 2 and 1.  

 

On April 21, we met the number 1 team, Bay Trees at their palatial, and impeccably maintained, facilities in Castro Valley.  As the sun went down, we were so cold that we were actually bundled up with the opposition.  Our ladies played good tennis, but we were ultimately beaten by better teams.  Our stars that day, Merris, Julia and Karen, delivered journeymen women performances.  The rest of us fought like the dickens but the final match score was them 3 us 2.

 

May 1 (May Day, flower day, etc) had a more desirable outcome.  Again, Karen and Julia were calm, collected and lethal playing the number one team.  These ladies were full of gamesmenslike tactics but did not win another game against our ladies after they won their third.  Hilarie dispatched her opponent with grace, style and beautiful tennis form. Merris met a seasoned veteran, and though she played well, ultimately went down to her.  Urs and I played two very tough opponents who may have well been Arlington's number one team.  Though we got to a tie breaker in the first set, we weren't able to convert and lost the second, also.  But Scap and Pauline came to the team's rescue.  I didn't see much of their match but I was impressed by what I saw and was thrilled with the outcome.

 

Lonnie

It is time for the National Night Out again. It is going to be from 6:00 to
8:00 PM on August 1, 2006.

The 23rd Annual National Night Out
Tuesday, August 1, 2006
Join the Tradition

How would you like to:

Improve the safety of your block
Have a fun time with your neighbors
Build community and take part in a huge, nationwide program?
You can do it all at this year’s National Night Out (NNO) on August 1! This
is a great time to get together with your neighbors at an event you design
just for your block. Your event will give neighbors a chance to get to know
each other better. When neighbors know and care about each other, they feel
more comfortable reporting crime and suspicious behavior to police. More
people watching out for each other and calling 911 will make your block a
safer place. And it all can start (or be recharged) with your NNO event!

NNO is a long tradition throughout the United States. In 2005, more than 34
million people from all 50 states got involved.

The location will be in our driveway, #30 and our neighbor's at #20. Bring
tables, chairs and goodies to share. Come and enjoy the falls!

Hi Sylvia, 

I'm glad you are able to attend next Friday.  We are pleased that you will be joining us for the Selection Center on Friday, June 13, 2008. 

Please arrive at 8:15 AM and we will conclude by 3:00 PM.  We will serve coffee and refreshments in the morning and lunch.  I will provide you with your interview schedule when you arrive.  The dress is business attire.  

The Selection Center will be held at 1800 Harrison Street, Oakland, CA on the 18th floor.  You will need to sign in at the security station in the lobby on the first floor before proceeding to the 18th floor.  Please be sure to bring a photo ID for security.  We will be gathering in the 18th floor reception area.   If you are driving, there is validated parking in the 1800 Harrison Street building only.  The entrance to the garage is on 17th street.

As mentioned, the day will consist of morning presentations by our senior leadership team, case study preparation time ( you will be given the case study after the morning presentations) and three one-on-one interviews that include case study feedback, technical interview and general HR interviews.

Sherry,

Maybe I wasn't clear in my original email.  The municipal code 15.52.060, Section A clearly states that the first cutting is to be shared 50%/50% by the tree owners and view claimant.  Subsequent costs to restore any view obstructed by the same tree or trees are to be borne 100% by the tree owner.  My neighbors and I  shared 50% of the cost in 1993 (see attached invoice; note that a 'sequoia sempervirens is a coastal redwood).  I paid for the same trees to be cut in 1997 (see attached invoices).    In 2002, my neighbor at the time, Bill Krause and I paid 100% of the subsequent cutting of the trees and others to restore our view.  These trees were trimmed by Gerry Smith, the arborist who did all the cutting since 1993.   On Gerry's invoice and picture, he clearly shows the level of where the trees were last cut.

Over the years, you and your board have taken a significant amount of time to get resolution for each cutting and I would like to see resolution occur quicker this time. I have borne more than my share to cut these redwoods and it is now the responsibility of Sereno to pay for subsequent cuttings.    I do not wish to be litigious but I will take the next step if your board insists that they are not paying for the cost to restore my view as it lawfully is the responsibility of the Sereno property board.  Please also bear in mind that losing in court will result in paying all attorney fees (Section 15.52.070) and costs of suit .  

I would hope that we do not have to go to the extent of meeting in court and can resolve this amicably and quickly.

Sylvia dos Remedios

HYPERLINK "mailto:SUtic@walshpm.com"SUtic@walshpm.com

Dear Sherry,

We are neighbors who live at 15 Kingwood Road, which is above your association’s development.  We purchased our home in 1991 and chose it because of the spectacular view of the Bay Area.  On several occasions, we have contacted with your association to top certain trees and bushes that had grown and impeded our view and impacted our property value.   The two Redwood trees next to #40 Sereno Circle that have been previously cut in 1993, 1997 and 2002, have grown to heights that impact our view.  

As you are aware, Oakland has a View Ordinance, which is Title 15, Chapter 15.52.  I have attached a copy of the Ordinance as well as the Amendment issued June 13, 2006.  The amendment was made to include trees that are allowed to sprout and grow as a result of natural regeneration in addition to trees that are planted by the owner which are already covered by the Ordinance.  While the Amendment does not impact this claim, I am including it so that you have the entire Ordinance Code related to views.

I would like to point out a few sections of the View Ordinance, Title 15:

1)      15.52.020 – Definitions:

a.       Natural Regeneration:  The trees in question are not considered natural regeneration but were in fact ‘planted’.

b.      Topping:  This was done previously to these trees.

c.       Tree Owner:  The Sereno Circle development is the owner of these trees

d.      View:  Specifies the view existing at the time a claimant acquired any property in the city.  Our view was unimpeded by trees or bushes when purchased in 1991.

2)      15.52.030 – Exemptions

a.       Section B.  Section B specifically indicates that the exemption is only for those Redwood trees that are present due to natural regeneration.   The trees in question were planted and therefore are not exempt from the view ordinance.

3)      15.52.050 – Standards for Resolution of Claims

a.       Section G (6):  Trimming, topping, thinning and tree removal must be performed by a licensed arborist.  I have attached a copy of an estimate from Gerald Smith, Certified Arborist #0429, W.C. ISA.  Please note that prior records indicate Mr. Smith was the arborist who topped the trees (as well as other bushes) in 1993, 1997 and 2002. He was also the arborist who topped several trees in 2010 that impacted the views of other Kingwood neighbors.

4)      15.52.060 – Apportionment of Costs

a.       Section A:  If a tree was planted subsequent to August 5, 1980 as these trees were, the initial costs are to be split 50%/50% between claimant and tree owner.

OBAMA ADMINISTRATION NAMES OPHELIA BASGAL AS HUD'S WESTERN REGIONAL DIRECTOR 

WASHINGTON - U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development Secretary Shaun Donovan today named Ophelia Basgal to serve as the Regional Director for HUD's Western Region. This region encompasses California, Arizona, Hawaii and Nevada.

Basgal is a nationally recognized expert on housing and community development issues, having served for 27 years as executive director of the Housing Authority of the County of Alameda, California. She also served on the Congressional Millennial Housing Commission and was a member of several national housing organizations. 

"Ophelia Basgal is a seasoned veteran who brings an in-depth understanding of housing and experience in building partnerships with community and civic groups," said Secretary Donovan. "During this time of transformation and outreach at HUD, Ophelia's skill set makes her a tremendous fit for this role."

"The chance to join HUD when it is retooling itself to meet the challenges of today's economy and housing market is a wonderful opportunity," said Basgal. "I look forward to joining a team that I believe will play an integral part in the effort to stabilize our communities and our housing market."

In her new position, Basgal will serve as HUD's liaison to mayors, city managers, elected representatives, state and local officials, congressional delegations, stakeholders and customers. She will also be responsible for overseeing the delivery of HUD programs and services to communities, and evaluating their efficiency and effectiveness.

Currently, Basgal serves as Vice President, Community Relations, for Pacific Gas and Electric Company where she is responsible for managing the company's $19.3 million charitable contributions program, employee volunteerism for PG&E's 20,000 employees and the community engagement programs. Ms. Basgal was also a board member of the University of California Puente Program, and the California Supreme Court Historical Society, where she served as vice-president. 

In 2009, Basgal, an Oakland resident for 35 years, earned the Ultimate Hispanic Executive award, an honor presented annually by the National Society of Hispanic MBAs to an outstanding Hispanic leader who demonstrates strength, integrity and commitment to public service, education, and the advancement of leadership.
Basgal holds a bachelor's degree in sociology from Arizona State University and a master's in social welfare administration from the University of California, Berkeley.

Congratulations! You have won one or more awards in the Destination Oakland Photo Contest. I just wanted to let you know that we have finalized the winners and the details for the public exhibit at the Oakland Convention & Visitors Bureau. The reception and exhibit will be on October 24, at the Oakland Marriott, from 5-7 p.m. We hope you will be able to make it and are invited to bring a guest. 

 

Initially we planned to announce the results at this reception, but it's taken longer than we expected to get the frames from Blicks and a date at the Marriott. With that in mind, we'd like to share the results with you ahead of time so we can distribute the checks (yea!) and I'd like to confirm the information you like next to your photos. 

 

I thought I'd see if anyone was available to meet for a casual lunch dowtown at Preservation Park (a pretty place for a group to meet outside) this Thursday or Friday at 12:30 pm. I can go through which photos won and you can let me know your preference for titles of the photos (some are titled in Flickr, some are not.) I can also give you details on the next contest theme and dates. The checks will be mailed out within the next two weeks. 

 

If you can't make it, we can go over it by email or phone. I just thought it might be fun to meet up ahead of the reception, which will include a broader group of people. 

 

Please let me know your availability for this Thursday (Sept 20) or Friday (Sept 21). If you'd like to discuss, my number is 510-864-0458. 

 

Thanks! 

 

Elisa Williams

Subject:        SAP Road Trip Confirmation! 

The 2008 SAP tournament is indeed shaping up to be an exciting, fun-filled event which will be featuring 8 of the top-ranked American male players, all who will be battling for the championship. Scheduled to play are Andy Roddick, James Blake, Mardy Fish, and German player, Tommy Haas. The Bryan Brothers, Davis Cup champions, lead the line-up in the doubles draw.

Thank you for signing up!  This is a second year teaming up with MTA and you are guaranteed to have a great time! 

The bus will be chartered, and will pick us up in the upper parking lot of the Mormon Temple in Oakland – and will leave promptly at 5:45 p.m. Bring food to share if you want. Park on the street to avoid having your car locked in the lot.  There are tons of food and beverage opportunities at the stadium as well.  

Please check in by 5:30 p.m.  Donna Haiman is the coordinator for MTA and she knows your name and will issue you a ticket as you board the bus!

FYI: Our seat locations are in the upper baseline, Section 206, rows 2,3,4, and 5. 

I am not able to attend this year.  If you should encounter any difficulties, please call me at 510-301-9427. 

Have a great time! 

Melissa

Hi Everyone
 
We have been invited to view a video Safeway put together for plans to change their site and the parking lot entrance to Lincoln Square among other plans.
 
The pastor at First Covenant was given the video and they want to make sure that any plans/changes have the communities blessing.  The pastor has invited us Sunday evening to preview the video:
 
Sunday 2/28 5:30pm at First Covenant
We are also invited to stay for an International Dinner that will be going on - there will be entertainment and it is a casual event.
 
No need to confirm/RSVP but I hope you can attend - this is an important issue.
 
If there are persons you know of that have expressed a direct interest in the Lincoln Square issue - please share this invite with them. I don't want to put it out on RHNA listserv as the venue is a bit small for that. We can share the information (maybe the video) at a later RHNA meeting. We are discussing zoning in April so perhaps then.
 
Seems like we should also make sure that Safeway knows how to contact us given the changes in store management in the last year.
 
Other thoughts?
 
cheers
Mary

Attachments:Kingwood Road Listing As Of 7-2-08.doc; _AVG certification_.txt

Greetings,

 

We had about 25 attendees at the meeting which was enjoyable and productive. We had two officers from the OPD. Officer Ann Pierce and officer Ricky Coglio. They were both knowledgeable and helpful.

 

We will make this an executive summary:

 

                The net result of helping to keep the street safe is to make a couple of signs that tells the world that they are under video surveillance. We pay for the signs and the city installs them.

                

                Light the street up as much as possible. Install Compact Fluorescent bulb and leave them on all night.

 

                Install a motion activated floodlight that is solar powered. I found one on the Target website for $90 and one on Home Depot’s site for $80. Only available online.

 

                Emergency telephone number if you are calling from a cell phone is 510-777-3211 IF YOU ARE CALLING FROM THE CITY OF OAKLAND. A 911 call from the cell phone goes to the CHP. 

 

                The non-emergency number from any phone is 510-777-3333. Calling 911 from a landline phone is still ok.

 

                If you see anyone who doesn’t belong in the neighborhood please call 911. Communicate through e-mail to your neighbors quickly.

 

                This is the worst fire season in memory. Please eliminate potential hazards such as overgrown brush near your house!

 

 

We will plan on meeting quarterly. The next meeting will be November 10, 2008 at 7 PM at our home. Please note this on your calendar.  If you have any specific topics that you would like to discuss, please let us know.

 

Please plan to bring a dessert or snack.

 

Thanks to all of you who brought food and drink last night.

 

Best regards,

 

Jerry and Esther

Description:
------------
From:Kathy Kramer [kathy@kathykramerconsulting.net]
Sent:Saturday, March 12, 2011 5:58 PM
To: HYPERLINK "mailto:Sylvia@meetThere.com" Sylvia@meetThere.com
Subject:Natives Garden Tour - registration information received

Thank you for registering for the Bringing Back the Natives Garden Tour. 

 - Your registration for the tour on Sunday, May 1 is complete. You will receive a guidebook in the mail by April 20th. If you have not received your guidebook by April 25th, please call Kathy at (510) 236-9558. 

 - If you have volunteered, you will be receiving your garden assignment via email by mid-April. 

 - this year, due to budget and time constraints, we will not be distributing fliers. If you would be willing to print and take fliers to your workplace, library, or coffee shop, download the file here: http://www.bringingbackthenatives.net/docs/2011-flier.pdf  We'd appreciate your help promoting native plant gardening by publicizing the tour. If you can forward a description of the tour to a friend, we'd be grateful. See the bottom of this email for text you can forward.

 - To ensure you receive all tour emails, add  HYPERLINK "mailto:kathy@kathykramerconsulting.net" kathy@kathykramerconsulting.net to your address book. 

Applications for the 2012 Tour are now being accepted. You can download the application form from the Tour website at http://bringingbackthenatives.net/garden-host-application.  Garden visits will be made in May and June. 

Volunteers are needed to spend a morning or afternoon greeting tour participants and answering questions about natives.  Complete the Volunteer section of the registration form if you would like to help out this year. Benefits to volunteers include: invitations to Garden Soirees, in which tour gardens are open to hosts and volunteers; a pre-tour meeting with the owner and private tour of the garden you will be staffing; a guaranteed tour reservation for the half day you are free; a Bringing Back the Natives Garden Tour t-shirt, and, perhaps best of all, heartfelt thanks for helping to educate the general public about the many pleasures and benefits of gardening with California native plants.  You can browse the website at http://bringingbackthenatives.net/preview-2011-gardens to choose the garden you would like to be assigned to. For more information, go here http://bringingbackthenatives.net/tour-volunteer-information,  e-mail Kathy Kramer at mailto:Kathy@KathyKramerConsulting.net, or 
call (510) 236-9558 between 9 am and 9 pm.


We hope you enjoy the Tour!



--- descriptive text for forwarding ---

The Bringing Back the Natives Garden Tour

Sunday, May 1, 2011  10am - 5pm

A free self-guided tour of native plant gardens in the East Bay. 

This delightful collection of gardens ranges from Jenny and Scott Fleming's 50-year-old collector's garden to brand new installations, from five acre lots to small front-yard gardens, from local native plants to natives from throughout California, and from gardens designed and installed by owners to those designed and installed by professionals.

For more information and to register for the tour: 

http://www.bringingbackthenatives.net
Referred by Dr. Maia Hightower’s NP (Alison Perry)\n\nPain in outer side of left foot.

Turn left on Oracle Parkway (first light), and park at the 600 Oracle Parkway Conference Center. There is a parking structure next to it. We look forward to seeing you. Cheers

Cinema Supper Club:

Berliner Film Fest

Five Weeks Only

October 23–November 20


Enjoy movies, dinner and art while avoiding weekend crowds with after-hours access to the special exhibition The State Museums of Berlin and the Legacy of James Simon.

Berliner Film Fest presents a five-week series of films made or set in Berlin: Metropolis (October 23), Cabaret (October 30), Grand Hotel (November 6), Run Lola Run (November 13), and Wings of Desire (November 20).

Cinema Supper Club also features two nights of live performances in the Legion Café at 6pm:


Opening Night, October 23: German cabaret music of the 1920s performed by Frederick Hodges
November 20: Le Jazz Hot, the new quartet of the Hot Club of San Francisco

Supper: A menu with German-inspired favorites such as Beer-braised Pork Sausage with Mashed Potatoes
and Sauerkraut and Wild Mushroom and Jarlsberg Pastry Pocket served on a bed of Sautéed Bloomsdale
Spinach are now available in the bar and dining room. Seatings from 6–8 pm, reservations recommended: 415.750.7633.

Cinema: Screenings being at 8 pm; exhibition open until 9 pm. Tickets: $20 ($10 members) for the movie and exhibition. For the exhibition only, admission is $15 (members are free). Now that you have had a chance to adjust to heading up Procurement, I hope that things are working out well for you and the department.  Based on the Chronicle article, it looks like Clorox is looking up, financially.

Been busy here at Shaklee since the sale of the company at the end of May.  It was purchased by an entrepreneur who wants to run the company more in that style than in traditional corporate style. In 1953, Marc Riboud moved to Paris where he joined the Magnum photo agency and traveled extensively, working with Magnum founders Henri Cartier-Bresson and Robert Capa.  His skill in capturing fleeting moments in life through powerful compositions, already apparent, was to serve him well for decades to come.

ISM-NC September 4, 2008 Dinner Meeting Announcement

 



SPECIAL EVENTS:

Honoring NAPM-NC Students of the year from CSU-East Bay 

   _____  

Organizational Change Management 

 

With 

Ellen Kumar

Regional Sales Director for RWD Technologies, LLC

 

Major Points Covered: 

 

The challenge of organizational change

1.    Typical organizational changes 

2.    Why it’s so hard and hurts so much 

3.    Why leaders and management need help 

 

Successful approaches to change

1.    You can’t contract this out 

2.    How to help and to be helped 

3.    In the end, the change has to be owned by those who stay 

 

Elements of a change management program or project

1.    Communications 

2.    Key stakeholder management 

3.    Leadership alignment (and coaching) 

4.    Organization and work design (or re-design) 

5.    Business readiness (engagement and transfer of ownership) 

6.    Project team issues and team building 

 

Final Thoughts

 

Questions, answers and lost and found items

 

 

Bio of the Speaker:  Ellen Kumar is a Regional Sales Director for RWD Technologies, LLC. (HYPERLINK "http://www.rwd.com/"www.rwd.com). RWD, headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland, is a leader in the field of human performance improvement consulting services and products. Ms. Kumar has worked for RWD since 1995, holding positions including training consultant, technical writer, project manager, and Western Region operations director, before moving into her current sales role in 2002. Prior to RWD Ms. Kumar worked as a Materials Engineer at the University of Dayton Research Institute and at General Electric Aircraft Engines (Evendale, OH). She has a Masters Degree in Materials Engineering from the University of Dayton, a Bachelors Degree in Metallurgical Engineering from the University of Cincinnati, and a Bachelors Degree in Applied Chemistry from Xavier University. Ms Kumar has presented on end user training and adoption, in the context of ERP implementations, at regional and national meetings of Americas’ SAP Users’ Group (ASUG).

Hello Ladies,  

 

Please come to the Shaklee Ladies Christmas Holiday Dinner on Monday, December 15th at 6:00 p.m.

 

I know that we had discussed and tentatively planned a dinner with a November date, but it was decided to instead make it a Holiday Christmas dinner and celebrate the season.  If you would like to participate, please bring an ornament for exchange and we'll have fun opening and  playing take away from each other.

 

The dinner will be at the "Black Angus" restaurant located in Dublin at 4814 Dublin Blvd.  Black Angus is in the same shopping center as the movie theatre, and is closest to the Best Buy store side of the shopping center.

 

Follow the link below to view the menu if you would like.

 

http://www.blackangus.com/menu_pdf/081027_Main3_TakeOut.pdf

Please RSVP by December 10th.

 

Thanks and look forward to seeing everyone!

 

Donna

Subject:***POSSIBLE SPAM::NAPM-NC DINNER MEETING JUNE 2 NOTE THE WEDNESDAY EVENING DATE - NEW MENU OPTIONS SEE BELOW

NAPM-NC June 2, 2004, "Wednesday" Evening Dinner Meeting Announcement
 
"E-commerce Supply Chain Management in Perspective"
 
Presented by 
Zinovy Radovilsky, Ph.D.
Professor of Management and Finance
California State University, Hayward

 
PROGRAM SUMMARY:
What is the status of e-commerce today after major cataclysmic changes happened in this area couple of years ago? How e-commerce is associated with supply chain and what value it adds to managing purchasing and sourcing decisions? What are the new perspective developments in e-commerce supply chain management including collaborative commerce and mobile commerce? These and other related questions will be addressed at the presentation. The speaker will present statistical and factual information on the status of e-commerce in supply chain management, describe major e-commerce models in supply chain, and provide information on perspectives and future development in this area.
 
SPECIAL EXTRA: Student award winners from Cal-State Hayward and Extension
 
Bio. of the Speaker:
Dr. Zinovy Radovilsky is a professor of Management in the College of Business and Economics at California State University, Hayward. Zinovy has been at the University since 1991. He teaches courses in production and service operations management, quantitative business methods, quality management, enterprise resource planning, and e-commerce. 
 
Zinovy has been a consultant in operations and supply chain management to leading U.S. companies and organizations such as APL Logistics, DHL Systems, Oread, EPRI, and others. 
 
Zinovy has done extensive research in the areas of enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, e-commerce supply chain, quality management, forecasting, and statistical analysis. He has published a number of papers in international and domestic journals such as International Journal of Management, International Journals of Operations and Production Management, International Journal of Production Economics, International Journal of Business Research, International Journal of Quality and Reliability Management, Journal of the Academy of Business and Economics, California Journal of Operations Management, Journal of Business Forecasting and others."

Sylvia,

 

I just realized that I have a hair appt. tomorrow night and am meeting my sister in Niles to have our hair done together; for some reason I thought the appt. was next week, and my Sis just reminded me it's tomorrow!  Can we meet after your vacation since I too will be gone a couple of extra days over the Labor day weekend (Going to Pismo Beach with my Sister) and then the family (Jeff, J.J. and I) are off to Hawaii for two weeks on September 30th  (yeah last family  vacation was several years ago!).   Are you able to meet the 3rd or 4th week in September?  

 

I can't find the work number that you left on my voice-mail, but think I have you cell phone on my cell, that is if it hasn't changed in six years!    I'll try calling you tonight on my way home.  So sorry to cancel our dinner tomorrow evening; hope we can re-schedule soon. 

 

BTW, I have your products, I was able to get the 2 cleansing lotions from Q.A. for free, the AM time lotion unfortunately is on backorder until October I'm told and Shaklee has discontinued our whole color line, so no more lipsticksL  I also have some freebee Vita-Lea that I can give to you when we meet.

Sorry, I never received your email at my home, did you use HYPERLINK "mailto:nunesfamily@att.net"nunesfamily@att.net ?  I have been going between Pleasanton and Hayward office this week so I just received your phone message.  I'd love to get together with you and it looks like Thursday August 26th will work for me also; let me know where you would like to meet, Pleasanton sounds good, we can do the Cheesecake Factory.

To: DNunes@shaklee.com
Subject: Hi from Sylvia

 

Hi Donna,


We haven't spoken in a long time!  I sent you an email awhile back to your home email but it came back as undeliverable.   Hope you are doing well!

 

Can you believe I've been at Kaiser two years this week!  Still can't see doing this for another three years but then again, can't come up with alternatives.

 

Going to NJ in about three weeks to see grandkids.  Unfortunately the day after I get back I have to do this major category review presentation so I'll probably spend my entire vacation learning the 'speech'.  It's a  big deal in Procurement and you do a category presentation to about 80 people including some top management.  The presentation is about 30 minutes with 15 minutes for Questions/Answers.

 

Anyway, let's figure out a time to get together and get caught up.  We can meet in Pleasanton if that works better for you.

 

Sylvia

Dear Steven,  

 

Exclusive Member Premiere: The Journey to Palomar
Saturday, October 25th at 7pm
Cost: Free to Members
Member Guest Special: $8.00 ticket price (general admission & premiere screening included)
 
You are invited to an exclusive preview screening of the new PBS award-winning documentary: HYPERLINK "http://rs6.net/tn.jsp?e=0019yjY1wVUL23e4RnVNVkNnOVKHUXrnGPSa6KadVeCbAmcghXFcKDvZjTDfkWemEzvP1WBsg3gw7UMengwPt2fCTrEVNcu0hq3qu_seQ2Ilsv-VkrOl7iwzC_TPJGR5T9m"The Journey to Palomar, premiering in Chabot's own MegaDome Theater.  This film tells the HYPERLINK "http://rs6.net/tn.jsp?e=0019yjY1wVUL22fyVRTR6YVozR49z0vGxiZAV_j3jMTpMAccDxzagsJvLc3u4hADDdAIwt4SMdbeeiMUoWgksvknFq1G9VChtfa4PNPrjBrkWGDfVYH0tnET9vjkpKJlaCAHK6RCH1U8ju5_2YWWYrseA=="Leahstory of George Ellery Hale, who built the biggest telescopes of the 20th century. More than a science film, HYPERLINK "http://rs6.net/tn.jsp?e=0019yjY1wVUL23e4RnVNVkNnOVKHUXrnGPSa6KadVeCbAmcghXFcKDvZjTDfkWemEzvP1WBsg3gw7UMengwPt2fCTrEVNcu0hq3qu_seQ2Ilsv-VkrOl7iwzC_TPJGR5T9m"The Journey to Palomar is the story of America's can-do spirit at its very best. Before we went to the Moon, Hale took us to the stars. Come view America's first journey into space.
 
Following the screening, you are invited to meet the film's producers, Robin & Todd Mason and experience Chabot's own dynamic history. Observe our Universe through Chabot's giant research-quality telescopes. Speak to our staff astronomers about the exciting history and future of telescopes, as well as the overall magic of astronomy.

Seating is limited & seats will be designated on a first-come first-served basis. Registration is required.

The Dana on Mission Bay is located near the beach in San Diego's Beach Cities neighborhood and close to Seaworld, Mission Beach, and Ocean Beach Park. Nearby points of interest also include Old Town San Diego State Park and Mission Bay Park. 

Hotel Features. 
The Dana on Mission Bay's restaurant serves breakfast, lunch, and dinner. A poolside bar and a bar/lounge are open for drinks. Room service is available during limited hours. Recreational amenities include 2 outdoor swimming pools and 2 spa tubs. Also located on site is a fitness facility. Spa amenities include massage/treatment rooms, facials, and beauty services. This 3.5-star property has a 24-hour business center and offers small meeting rooms, audio-visual equipment, and business services. Complimentary wireless Internet access is available in public areas. This San Diego property has event space consisting of banquet facilities and a ballroom. Complimentary shuttle services include a theme park shuttle and a train station pick-up service. Wedding services, concierge services, and tour/ticket assistance are available. Guest parking is available for a surcharge. Additional property amenities include a marina, multilingual staff, and gift shops/newsstands. This is a smoke-free property. 

Guestrooms. 
271 air-conditioned guestrooms at The Dana on Mission Bay feature MP3 docking stations and coffee/tea makers. Rooms are all accessible via exterior corridors. Accommodations offer pool, garden, or water views. Beds come with triple sheeting and down blankets. Refrigerators and microwaves are offered. Bathrooms feature bathrobes, complimentary toiletries, and hair dryers. Wireless Internet access is complimentary. Guestrooms offer phones with voice mail. High-definition televisions have satellite channels and pay movies. Also included are windows that open and electronic check-out. Housekeeping is offered daily and guests may request wake-up calls. Guestrooms are all non-smoking.

Hi Everyone,
 
Great to see all the fun ideas flying around.  I've got a couple more to add.  I know we already have our December event from Jack and Ronnie but since this is happening I thought I'd throw it out as well.
 
There is a lighted yacht parade on the estuary between Jack London Square and Alameda on Saturday, December 5, starting at 5:30p.  The theme is Christmas Carol's.  The Lighted Yacht Parade partners with The Oakland Firefighters Random Acts and the Alameda County Community Food Bank. Participants and spectators are encouraged to donate food, new unwrapped toys, or money at designated drop sites located at Encinal Yacht Club and Oakland Yacht Club. These donations go a long way toward providing food and toys to deserving kids and families during the holiday season.
 
We could make reservations at one of the waterfront restaurants, pitch a picnic on one of the grassy areas, make reservations to sail on the Ferry boat in the parade, or just stroll about.  
 
http://www.lightedyachtparade.com/index.html
 
Not having been to this event before I'm not sure of the Fun Factor and the weather will make a difference if we're outside.
 
 
The other idea to add to the mix (we're still settling on dates for bowling and/or Flamenco I believe) is Campo di Bocce of Livermore (instead of beer and pizza when we bowl it's wine and cheese while we bocce!).

They have eight World Class, State-of-the-Art Bocce courts, traditional Italian Cuisine, and a well appointed bar to fulfill any thirst...carry a large variety of local Livermore Valley and California wines and a plethora of imported wines...Lunch, Dinner, and Daily Bocce.  A fun Saturday or Sunday afternoon excursion.  http://www.campodibocce.com/Livermore/index.html.  

If anyone is interested in the Yacht Parade let me know and get to organizing.  

Barbara

Hello Ladies,  

 

Please come join us for the 2nd annual Shaklee Ladies Christmas Holiday Dinner on Friday, December 10th at 6:00 p.m.

 

The dinner will be held at Dino's Restaurant located in Castro Valley at 3600 Castro Valley Blvd., phone

Keeping with the spirit of the Christmas season, we would again like to have a gift exchange.   Please bring a wrapped ornament for exchange valued at no more than $10.  When your number comes up, and if you do not wish to open a wrapped ornament because you fancy one that has already been unwrapped, you may steal that ornament.  An ornament can only be stolen three times before it is no longer in play.

National Night Out

 

What is National Night Out?
National Night Out is the nation's night out against crime. Sponsored by the National Association of Town Watch, residents are encouraged to join with their neighbors to have a neighborhood block party, ice cream social or other outdoor event to build neighborhood spirit and unity - which is the first defense against crime. Research shows that when neighbors know each other and look out for each other crime goes down.

 

The Kingwood Road Neighborhood Watch will be holing a block party on August 5, 2008 from 7-9 PM in front of number 20 (the Kessler home) again. Please bring something to share along with a chair. A good time, great fun and getting to know your neighbors!

 

We will be paid a visit by the OPD. Usually they pass out some goodies for kids and adults alike. MARK YOUR CALENDAR FOR AUGUST 5TH!

 



  
Best regards,

 


Jerry & Esther Moran

Description:
It’s time to give back, Yelp-style! Rock on down to the David Brower Center for the East Bay’s first Yelp Do-Good Event:
Yelp Helps!
Everyone’s invited! Well, you and a guest are invited to the David Brower Center on Monday, October 18th, 2010, from 7:30-9:30pm for a night filled with amazing non-profits, local food, crafty cocktails and live entertainment!
Over 30 non-profits will be on-site to promote volunteer opportunities and ways to give back. While you learn more from the participating organizations, a dazzling array of local restaurants and beverage stations will be on-hand to tantalize your taste buds and quench your thirst. 
Heather the magician will be showing off a mind-bending magic, Chixx Mixx (an all-girl band formed at the Bay Area Girl’s Rock program) will perform live, and other entertainers will be strewn throughout the space. 
Additional sponsors are still being finalized, so stay tuned to the event listing for more details. But, in the meantime start spreading the good word!

Summary: Hapgood Reading of Tom Stoppard's play
Description:
HYPERLINK "https://www.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=anYwN3RwbzljbTZvMGhjbmRxbXJkNHVpMjggc2Rvc3JlbWVkaW9zQG0&tok=MjMjc3Nkb3NyZW1lZGlvc0BnbWFpbC5jb21jYWFlNTA4ZmE3ZDE3MDhmOGFkYjQxZTE5MjNlZWM4NWQ2ZTg0NzIz&ctz=America/Los_Angeles&hl=en"more details »


Hapgood Reading of Tom Stoppard's play

Arcadia is proving to be such a hit that we are adding an extension week! In huge gratitude for your support of this show, we want to invite you to a special free reading of Tom Stoppard’s 1988 spy thriller, Hapgood.

The reading will take place on Tuesday, June 11, at 7 p.m. at The Geary Theater, and it will feature the extraordinary company of actors from Arcadia. To RSVP for this event, please email HYPERLINK "mailto:rsvp@act-sf.org"rsvp@act-sf.org by Monday, June 10, at 5 p.m. Seating is limited, and will be general admission. You will receive a confirmation once you RSVP.

About the play:
Hapgood, a lesser-known work of Stoppard’s oeuvre directly preceding Arcadia in his timeline, focuses on a British female spymaster and her Russian counterpart—who is also the father of her child. Not only is the play a dizzying political game, like Arcadia it incorporates scientific concepts, revolving around Heisenberg’s principle of uncertainty to trace sets of twins who are sending secrets to Moscow. 

I so hope you’ll join us for this fun script-in-hand reading as we explore this truly unique and rarely heard play from the Stoppard canon.
